tier_justification: 1 work-unit, no irreversible/outward surface (local dashboard helper), but no existing test covers the goal state today (that absence is the bug) — tier 0's exemption predicate does not hold, so this takes the tier 1 standard set.

evals.assert: pressButton resolves to { ok: false, reason: 'invalid-input' } (never rejects/throws) when buildArgv throws on empty-command-plus-no-input, and no fs/spawn side effect fires
evals.cmd: cd skills/dashboard/obsidian && npx vitest run test/exec.test.ts -t 'buildArgv throw parity'
evals.negative_control: git stash the try/catch wrap in src/exec.ts (revert to unwrapped buildArgv call), re-run the same command — must fail (unhandled rejection) since exec.ts:126 propagates the throw uncaught on origin/main today
evals.evidence: Pre-fix (RED, confirmed live): 'Error: buildArgv: refusing to spawn an empty prompt (no command and no input)' thrown at argv.ts:58, uncaught through exec.ts:126, test failed for the right reason. Post-fix (GREEN): npx vitest run test/exec.test.ts -t 'buildArgv throw parity' -> 'Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 2 passed | 16 skipped (18)'.

evals.assert: pressButton resolves to { ok: false, reason: 'invalid-input' } (never rejects/throws) when input has leading whitespace then a dash (slips exec.ts's own untrimmed pre-check, hits buildArgv's trimmed check), and no fs/spawn side effect fires
evals.cmd: cd skills/dashboard/obsidian && npx vitest run test/exec.test.ts -t 'buildArgv throw parity'
evals.negative_control: git stash the try/catch wrap in src/exec.ts, re-run the same command — must fail since exec.ts:116's untrimmed startsWith('-') check does not catch '  --dangerously-skip-permissions' and the throw propagates uncaught
evals.evidence: Pre-fix (RED, confirmed live): 'Error: buildArgv: input must not start with '-' (got:   --dangerously-skip-permissions)' thrown at argv.ts:66, uncaught through exec.ts:126, test failed for the right reason (exec.ts:116's untrimmed check let it through). Post-fix (GREEN): same run as E1 -> 2 passed.

evals.assert: full obsidian test directory (config/notes/render/exec) stays green after the fix — no regression
evals.negative_control: reintroduce a deliberate syntax error in exec.ts's catch block — the suite must fail to run/compile, proving the runner actually executes this code
evals.evidence: npm test -> 'Test Files 4 passed (4), Tests 46 passed (46)' (baseline pre-change was 4 files / 44 tests; +2 from this PR's new RED/GREEN tests, 0 regressions).

evals.assert: no new PressResult.reason union member was added beyond the existing 'invalid-input' (reuse, not proliferation) unless a review finding argues otherwise
evals.negative_control: add a fake 4th reason literal to the PressResult union and re-run the grep — must find and report it, proving the grep isn't vacuously empty
